content:
I'm trying to make my own custom kernel module in rust as a hobby project. Since I want to keep using the same module source across kernel updates, I'm trying to use kernel header and rust kernel library packages supplied by my package manager.
Specifically, I use ubuntu LTS with kernel 6.11.0-25-generic installed. I installed "linux-hwe-6.11-lib-rust-6.11
.0-25-generic" and I'm trying to develop kernel module out of tree.
The template works well, but I have trouble generating bindings other than what was already provided. I want to read power supply information(i.e. power_supply_get_by_name) but I don't have bindings_helper.h
I need that header file as it is specified in linux official documentary:

By including a C header from include/ into rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h, the bindgen tool will auto-generate the bindings for the included subsystem. After building, see the *_generated.rs output files in the rust/bindings/ directory.
We do not yet have a way to generate your own bindings out of tree.
I see. I'm fine with building whole kernel but I don't want to use specific kernel just to have my kernel module installed.
Rust modules, like C modules, are only compatible with the kernel that you used to build it.

Alice Ryhl said:

Rust modules, like C modules, are only compatible with the kernel that you used to build it.

Unless you are using a fork with a stable ABI, such as RHEL.
